title: App crashes on login with malformed JWT token
body:
## Description

When a user sends a malformed JWT token to the `/api/auth/login` endpoint, the application crashes with an unhandled `NullPointerException`. This allows unauthenticated users to cause a denial-of-service by repeatedly sending bad tokens.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Send a POST request to `/api/auth/login` with `Authorization: Bearer INVALID.TOKEN.HERE`
2. Observe the server returns a 500 Internal Server Error
3. Check logs for `NullPointerException` in `JWTValidator.java:42`

## Expected Behavior

The server should return a 401 Unauthorized response with a clear error message.

## Environment

- Version: 2.4.1
- OS: Ubuntu 22.04
- Java: 17
author: alice

title: Authentication bypass via null token header
body:
## Description

Similar to other auth issues reported. If the `Authorization` header is set to `null` as a string literal, the JWT validation is skipped entirely and the user is granted admin-level access.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Send `Authorization: null` header to any protected endpoint
2. Observe that access is granted without valid credentials

## Impact

Full authentication bypass — any user can access admin endpoints.
author: bob

title: Database connection pool exhausted under high load
body:
## Description

Under sustained load (>500 concurrent users), the PostgreSQL connection pool is exhausted, causing all new requests to fail with `ConnectionPoolTimeoutException`. The service becomes completely unavailable until manually restarted.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run load test with `k6 run --vus 600 --duration 60s load_test.js`
2. After ~30 seconds, all requests start returning 503 errors
3. Pool exhaustion confirmed in metrics dashboard

## Expected Behavior

The application should gracefully handle connection limits and queue requests or return appropriate error responses.

## Metrics

- Pool size: 20
- Peak connections: 600
- Timeout: 5000ms
author: carol

title: Memory leak in WebSocket connection handler
body:
## Description

The WebSocket connection handler does not properly release resources when a client disconnects unexpectedly. Over time, this causes memory usage to grow unboundedly, eventually triggering OOM kills.

## Observed Behavior

- Memory increases by ~50MB per hour under normal load
- After 48 hours, the pod is OOM-killed by Kubernetes
- After restart, memory growth resumes immediately

## Investigation

Heap dumps show a large number of `WebSocketSession` objects being retained in `ConnectionRegistry` even after the client has disconnected. The `onClose` handler does not call `registry.remove(session.getId())`.
author: dave

title: User profile picture upload silently fails for PNG files > 2MB
body:
## Description

When a user attempts to upload a profile picture that is a PNG file larger than 2MB, the upload appears to succeed (HTTP 200 returned) but the image is never saved. The user's old profile picture remains unchanged with no error message shown.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Go to Settings > Profile
2. Click 'Change Profile Picture'
3. Select a PNG file larger than 2MB
4. Click Upload
5. Observe: success message is shown but picture is unchanged

## Expected Behavior

Either the image should be saved, or a clear error message should explain the size limit.
author: eve

title: Password reset link not expiring after use
body:
## Description

Password reset links remain valid even after they have been used to reset a password. This means an attacker who intercepts a used reset link could still use it to take over the account.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Request a password reset
2. Use the reset link to change password
3. Use the same reset link again
4. Observe: the link still works and allows another password change

## Impact

High security risk — used reset tokens should be invalidated immediately after first use.
author: frank

title: Search results not paginating correctly after 100 results
body:
## Description

When a search query returns more than 100 results, navigating to page 2 returns duplicate results from page 1 instead of the next batch. Pages 3 and beyond return empty results.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Search for a common term (e.g. 'user')
2. Scroll to the bottom of page 1
3. Click 'Next Page'
4. Observe: same results as page 1 are shown

## Root Cause (suspected)

The pagination cursor is not being persisted correctly between requests. The `offset` parameter appears to be reset to 0 on each request.
author: grace

title: API rate limiting not applied to /api/v1/export endpoint
body:
## Description

The `/api/v1/export` endpoint, which generates and returns large CSV exports, is not subject to rate limiting. A single user can hammer this endpoint repeatedly, consuming excessive CPU and memory and degrading service for all other users.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Obtain a valid API token
2. Call `GET /api/v1/export?type=full` in a tight loop
3. Observe: no rate limit errors returned, server CPU spikes to 100%

## Expected Behavior

Rate limiting should be applied consistently to all API endpoints.
author: henry

title: Dark mode toggle not persisted across sessions
body:
## Description

When a user enables dark mode, the preference is correctly applied for the current session. However, after logging out and logging back in (or refreshing with a cleared session), the dark mode preference is reset to light mode.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Log in
2. Enable dark mode via Settings > Appearance
3. Log out
4. Log back in
5. Observe: dark mode is disabled
author: iris

title: CSV export contains corrupted Unicode characters in user-generated content
body:
## Description

When exporting data that contains Unicode characters (emoji, CJK characters, Arabic text) in user-generated content fields, the exported CSV file contains corrupted or replaced characters (typically `?` or garbled sequences).

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a record with Unicode content (e.g., title: "Hello 世界 🌍")
2. Export data as CSV
3. Open the CSV in Excel or a text editor
4. Observe: Unicode characters are corrupted

## Root Cause (suspected)

The CSV writer is likely using Latin-1 encoding instead of UTF-8. The export should use UTF-8 with BOM for Excel compatibility.
author: jack
